Genital warts 
Genital warts are small, rough lumps that can appear around the vagina, penis or anus. They're a common sexually transmitted infection (STI). Treatment from a sexual health clinic can help. 
Symptoms of genital warts 
It can take a few weeks or months for genital warts to appear after having sex with someone who is infected.
Genital warts are usually small, rough lumps on the skin.
They can appear:
around the vagina and on the penis around the anus on the skin between the genitals and anus (perineum) Genital warts may:
appear on their own or in a group (groups of warts can look like a cauliflower) feel soft or firm be white, red, skin-coloured, or darker than the surrounding skin They do not usually cause symptoms but sometimes they may be painful, itchy or bleed.
You may also have:
pain or discomfort during sex problems peeing (there may be blood in your pee, you may find it hard to pee, or you may stop and start a lot)
